Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The DECLARE model is a Python dictionary with specific keys The keys are divided into two groups The first group includes existence absence exactly one init and for these the value is a dict where keys are activities and values are support 1 0 and confidence The second group has all the other keys like responded existence etc

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For binary relations like response the key would need to be a pair of activities For example for response A B the key would be A B

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Looking at pm4py documentation though I might not have it here but based on common practice in DECLARE models the constraints for binary relations are specified with tuples of two activities

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The scenario describes a process where each product idea goes through a series of steps IG to DD to TFC to CE to PC to LT to UT to AG to MP to FL So the typical flow is a sequence of these activities in order

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a product design process certain steps must happen in order some are optional etc

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible constraints init IG must be the first activity succession between consecutive steps and existence for each activity meaning each activity must occur at least once

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Each product idea goes through all steps so existence for all activities and exactly one for some steps not sure but since it is a linear process each activity is done once per product idea

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait chainresponse would be that A is immediately followed by B But perhaps chainsuccession is more appropriate because it means A is immediately followed by B and B is immediately preceded by A

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Chainsuccession IG DD would mean IG is immediately followed by DD and DD is immediately preceded by IG which is true in the linear sequence

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Given that the process is a strict sequence the main constraints are the chainsuccession between consecutive steps init exactly one and existence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The DECLARE model dict will have all the keys listed in the prompt but for keys that have no constraints their value is an empty dict

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The final DECLARE model includes existence for all activities exactly one for all activities init for IG and chainsuccession for each consecutive pair All other constraint types have empty dicts
